title: FindingNemo Library 2: Modified RAD004
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"37jk","text":"This sub-protocol is designed to prepare library from extracted ultra-high molecular weight (UHMW) DNA to obtain ultra-long (UL) reads on Nanopore sequencers. The UL library protocol we tested here is based on ONT's rapid kit, i.e., SQK-RAD004, a transposase based adapter ligation kit.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":227,"length":6},{"style":"bold","offset":233,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e5d50","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7g7ps","text":"This Modified RAD004 protocol consistently produced N50 \u003e 100 kb from a good input quality of UHMW DNA, for when ULK001 is not accessible/available. Transposase-based reaction is done in a large volume of up to 1 ml.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":5,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eaq5v","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e0c32","text":"The working principle of the RAD004 kit is shown in the diagram below:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bbq1j","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"uwbhf","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"528h9","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"image","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"6235A134341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","height":228.24999999999991,"is_video":false,"legend":"{\"blocks\":[{\"key\":\"fmh6v\",\"text\":\"source: https://store.nanoporetech.com/uk/rapid-sequencing-kit.html\",\"type\":\"unstyled\",\"depth\":0,\"inlineStyleRanges\":[],\"entityRanges\":[{\"key\":0,\"offset\":8,\"length\":59}],\"data\":{}}],\"entityMap\":{\"0\":{\"type\":\"link\",\"mutability\":\"\",\"data\":{\"guid\":\"8C3A46D20CA711ECA7CD0A58A9FEAC02\",\"url\":\"https://store.nanoporetech.com/uk/rapid-sequencing-kit.html\"}}}}","mime":"image/png","nestedSelector":null,"original_name":"rapid.png","placeholder":"https://content.protocols.io/files/echv7t5n.jpg","shadow":false,"source":"https://content.protocols.io/files/echs7t5n.jpg","webp_placeholder":"https://s3.amazonaws.com/protocols-files/files/echu7t5n.webp","webp_source":"https://content.protocols.io/files/echp7t5n.webp","width":260.85714285714283}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"3s6j1","text":"Acknowledgements","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c3g2m","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cjve3","text":"The FindingNemo protocol series was developed by Inswasti Cahyani for the Long Read Club with significant contributions from John Tyson and Nadine Holmes, also Josh Quick, and continuous discussion and support of Matt Loose and Nick Loman. We would also like to thank Giron Koetsier (NEB) and Kelvin Liu (Circulomics) for lending their expertise and advance product trials. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5qbpm","text":"Make up buffers with nuclease-free water to avoid introducing nucleases to solutions. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7415c","text":"Avoid unnecessary heating and freezing; isolated DNA should be stable for storage in the fridge for months","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Extracted UHMW DNA is often difficult to quantify due to its viscosity. However, accurate measurement of DNA concentration is crucial for calculating optimum ratio of the transposase enzyme to the DNA molecules. We provide a protocol section for quantifying UHMW DNA in our 'FindingNemo' protocol master file. Properly quantified DNA can then be processed for this library prep. Both cell number and DNA concentration/amount are used to calculate the amount of transposase (FRA) and adapter (RAP). We base this protocol on SQK-RAD004 but follow the SQK-ULK001 protocol for the optimum ratio of transposase amount to human genomic DNA: 6 μl FRA to 6 million human cells (or around 40 μg DNA) For other species, the genome size has to be taken into account and the FRA to DNA ratio optimised, e.g., we had optimised a non-human cell line of 6.2 Gb genome at: 2.5 μl FRA to 1 million non-human cell (around 12-15 μg DNA)

The following table lists the replaced reagents between ULK001 and RAD004. A B C 1 Reagent ULK001 RAD004 (modified) 2 Dilution buffer FDB MuA buffer (see Materials) 3 Transposase FRA* FRA^ 4 Sequencing adapter RAP-F RAP (*) FRA enzyme in ULK001 kit (^) FRA enzyme in RAD004 kit

In a 2 ml tube, dilute UHMW DNA to a concentration of around 50 ng/μl in a total volume of 750 μl (with water or elution buffer if required). Mix well with a P1000 wide-bore tip. DNA concentration can still range from 20-50 ng/μl to have optimum transposase reaction. If input DNA amount is less than 20 μg (1-3 million cells used), halve all the reaction volumes, i.e., 375 μl total DNA volume instead of 750 μl as in the table below. It is important to have as homogeneous DNA as possible at this step so the transposase can access and cut the DNA molecules with an even distribution. It is OK to pipette thoroughly but gently. A B C D E 1 Cell No. (million) Approx. DNA amount (μg) Total DNA volume (μl) DNA concentration (ng/μl) Total reaction volume (μl) 2 6 >20-40 750 20-50 1000 3 5 4 4 5 3 5-20 375 500 6 2 7 1

In a 1.5 ml tube, dilute the corresponding amount of transposase (FRA) with MuA buffer to a total volume of 250 μl (or 125 μl if doing half-reaction). More details in the table below. A B C D E 1 Cell No. (million) Approx. DNA amount (μg) FRA (μl) 4X MuA Buffer (μl) Total reaction volume (μl) 2 6 >20-40 6 244 1000 3 5 5 245 4 4 4 246 5 3 5-20 3 122 500 6 2 2 123 7 1 1 124
Mix the diluted FRA by vortexing for 2-3 seconds.

Using a P1000 wide-bore tip, add the diluted FRA to the DNA sample. Stir the reaction with the pipette tip whilst expelling the diluted FRA to ensure an even distribution. Mix thoroughly by gentle pipetting. On ice

Incubate the reaction as follows: 23 °C 00:10:00 70 °C 00:05:00 Room temperature 00:10:00, at least It is important that the room temperature at the fragmentation step (first incubation step) does not fall below 20 o C to ensure optimum reaction condition. The use of a water bath or heating block is recommended.

Add the corresponding volume of sequencing adapter (RAP) as in the table below. A B C D E 1 Cell No. (million) Approx. DNA amount (μg) FRA (μl) RAP (μl) Total reaction volume (μl) 2 6 >20-40 6 5 1000 3 5 5 4.2 4 4 4 3.3 5 3 5-20 3 2.5 500 6 2 2 1.7 7 1 1 0.8 Use a P1000 wide-bore tip to pipette mix. Visually check to ensure the reaction is thoroughly mixed. Tube inversion can be used to aid mixing.
Incubate for 30 minutes at 23 o C. 23 °C 00:30:00
creator: Inswasti Cahyani
